hero.question: Will Novak Djokovic be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
hero.prob_take: At 8.6%, the crowd is pricing Djokovic as a historical longshot for 2026 Wimbledon, essentially writing off a 39-year-old who hasn't won a major since 2023 and may not return to peak grass-court form. The real question is whether 8.6% fairly compensates for his injury history, the grueling tour grind, and the four-year gap since his last Grand Slam win, or if it's genuinely too generous to a player whose best years are demonstrably behind him.

daily_take.headline: Djokovic's Wimbledon Odds Jump 3 Points. Market Pricing in Retirement Risk.
daily_take.deck: Novak's odds climbed to 8.6% overnight on $494K volume, a sharp move that signals real money questioning whether the 37-year-old will even compete in 2026. The market is right to be skeptical, but 8.6% undervalues his historical dominance on grass. A sharp bettor takes the under on his age being the deciding factor before May 2026.
